replication_factor.description: The number of replicas of each partition. A replication factor of 3 is recommended for high availability.

location.description: ID of the location of the Kafka resource. See https://cloud.google.com/managed-kafka/docs/locations for a list of supported locations.

topic_id.description: The ID to use for the topic, which will become the final component of the topic's name. This value is structured like: `my-topic-name`.

partition_count.description: The number of partitions in a topic. You can increase the partition count for a topic, but you cannot decrease it. Increasing partitions for a topic that uses a key might change how messages are distributed.

configs.description: Configuration for the topic that are overridden from the cluster defaults. The key of the map is a Kafka topic property name, for example: `cleanup.policy=compact`, `compression.type=producer`.

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
